content: A domain with 81 invariants (34 non-negotiable + 26 craft-tool conditional + 21 process/structural) is manageable for a 67-agent system when invariants are distributed across phase auditors. The invariant-per-agent reference count followed a bell-curve distribution (median ~5 references per invariant). However, conditional invariants (craft-tool 'when selected' rules) require special routing logic: the craft-profile-selector agent's output must be readable by all downstream auditors so they know which tools to enforce. This was a key architectural decision that worked well but required 4 gap-hunting cycles to fully validate all enforcement paths.
sourceEvidence: 81 invariants in domain-model.json. architecture.json convergence.expectedCycles: 'Fourth cycle is insurance for the craft-tool conditional invariants (26 When selected invariants requiring craft-profile-aware routing).' gap-report Category 2: all 81 invariants verified with distribution from 1-67 references.

history.reason: Cold start — first observation from initial factory run
